model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: Gonzaga is the clear pick because the underlying team-quality gap is large: the Bulldogs rate No. 11 nationally in both KenPom and Torvik, with a 30-3 record and top-10 caliber adjusted defense, while Kennesaw State profiles more like a true mid-major underdog and entered as roughly a 20.5-point dog. The Owls do have a credible path if they turn this into a high-variance shooting game after their conference tournament run, but Gonzaga’s much stronger efficiency profile, tougher schedule, and no surfaced major availability concern make the Bulldogs the far safer advance choice.

reasoningStep.title: Gonzaga over Kennesaw State
reasoningStep.summary: Gonzaga’s predictive profile and overall résumé are far stronger, and I did not find a meaningful injury or availability issue that would materially narrow the gap.
evidence: KenPom and Torvik both rate Gonzaga No. 11 nationally; Gonzaga is 30-3 with roughly a +28 adjusted efficiency margin, about 122 adjusted offensive efficiency, and about 94 adjusted defensive efficiency.
Web search results indicate Gonzaga was around a 20.5-point favorite, consistent with a substantial class gap for a 3-vs-14 matchup.

Kennesaw State reached the field as Conference USA tournament champion and has an upset path through variance and shot-making, but available local and web sources did not surface a stronger underlying profile than Gonzaga’s.
ESPN news search did not return a major injury storyline for either side, and other web checks did not reveal a significant availability concern that changes the pick.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
